items.title: Scale features in a pipeline
items.avoid: Fitting k-NN on raw mixed-scale numeric columns.
items.prefer: StandardScaler (zero mean, unit variance) or domain-appropriate scaling inside sklearn Pipeline before KNeighborsClassifier.
items.why: Distance is only meaningful when feature units contribute comparably to ||x − xᵢ||.

items.title: Tune k on validation data
items.avoid: Fixing k = 3 or k = 5 by habit without measuring validation error.
items.prefer: Sweep odd k values (or grid with weights/metrics) on a held-out set or cross-validation; pick minimum validation loss.
items.why: Optimal k depends on n, noise, and class overlap — ties directly to bias–variance tradeoff in Model Complexity.
items.example: for k in range(1, 31, 2): score on val; plot accuracy vs k.

items.title: Define tie-breaking for classification
items.avoid: Assuming sklearn or your code handles ambiguous votes silently in all edge cases.
items.prefer: Use odd k for binary problems, weights='distance', or explicit smallest-distance tie-break documented in production.
items.why: Equal votes among top classes create inconsistent predictions across library versions or tie order.
items.example: Binary task: n_neighbors=5 instead of 4; document tie policy in API spec.

items.title: Match k to problem size
items.avoid: Using k = 1 on noisy labels or k ≈ n on small clean datasets.
items.prefer: Start near k = √n for classification as a heuristic, then validate; use larger k when label noise is high.
items.why: k = 1 memorizes noise; k too large washes out local structure needed for accurate boundaries.
items.example: n = 400 training points → try k ∈ {7, 9, 11, 13, 15} on validation.

items.title: Plan for inference cost at deployment
items.avoid: Shipping exact linear-scan k-NN on full historical data without latency testing.
items.prefer: Profile query time; use algorithm='ball_tree' or 'kd_tree' when d is moderate, or subsample / ANN index when n is huge.
items.why: Training is cheap but prediction cost grows with stored training set size.
items.example: KNeighborsClassifier(algorithm='ball_tree', leaf_size=30) after scaling low-d tabular data.
